result.name: Latin American and Latino Studies Minor
result.templateAssets:
[{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals.


Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

","groupId":"90c86f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR","groupId":"03fd7845-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"GENERAL INFORMATION FOR ALL MINORS","groupId":"da746f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Students majoring in a different program of study may find that a minor in LALS is an invaluable addition to their educational plan. Prospective transfer students interested in minoring in LALS are encouraged to complete two lower-division LALS courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring; see the “Preparation for the Minor” section below.


Please visit the Latin American and Latino Studies (LALS) website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Latin American and Latino Studies Minor Program Statement


Prospective transfer students who would like to connect with an LALS advisor are strongly encouraged to email lalsadvising@ucsc.edu.
